mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment above 145k signals strong labor market demand, driving consumer spending and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in PAYEMS >145k periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with declining unemployment indicate strong demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in regimes with HOUST>1200 and UNRATE<4.5 are negative or IC < 0.04 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged increase in CPI above 2.5% signals inflation persistence, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in CPI>2.5 periods are positive or IC < -0.03 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment indicates weakening supply-side capacity and demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in INDPRO<105 and UNRATE>4.3 regimes are positive or IC > -0.04 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction combined with rising housing starts signals mixed demand dynamics, creating volatility that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in GDPC1<0 and HOUST>1200 regimes are positive or IC > -0.03 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment below 4.0% signals labor market tightness, increasing wage pressures that reduce corporate margins and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns in UNRATE<4.0 periods are positive or IC > -0.02 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable CPI signals strong supply-side growth and controlled inflation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in INDPRO>105 and CPIAUCSL<2.4 regimes are negative or IC < 0.03 in 3-year windows.
